name: @trailblaze/scripting
description: TypeScript SDK for authoring Trailblaze MCP tools — wraps @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with the Trailblaze `_meta.trailblaze` envelope reader and a `trailblaze.tool`/`trailblaze.run` authoring surface. The host-side type surface that ships in the framework JAR is `./dist/index.d.ts` — a self-contained declaration bundle (zod types inlined via dts-bundle-generator) that the framework extracts into `<workspace>/.trailblaze/sdk/dist/` so per-trailmap tsconfigs can point at it without any `node_modules/` step. The bundle is committed under `dist/` and CI's `verifyTrailblazeSdkDtsBundle` re-runs the generator and byte-diffs to catch source drift. `package.json` `main`/`types` still point at `./src/index.ts` — that's both the runtime entry point AND the input dts-bundle-generator resolves `declare module "@trailblaze/scripting"` self-augmentations against. Authors who actually want to RUN the SDK (e.g. the sample-app example) consume `./src/index.ts` via a TS-capable runtime (bun/tsx) through a `file:` link.
